MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Jan-14-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Keith E. Patterson of YARON & ASSOCIATES (415-658-2929), appearing for defendant / moving party Puget Sound Commerce Center F/K/A Todd Shipyards Corporation
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Michael Rhodes

RULING:
DEFENDANT PUGET SOUND COMMERCE CENTER, INC. F/K/A TODD SHIPYARDS CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Puget Sound Commerce Center, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Privette v. Superior Court (1993) 5 Cal.4th 689, and its progeny, bar plaintiff's claims against it. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and special interrogatory responses preclude defendant from sustaining its initial burden because defendant's alleged liability is based on the affirmative conduct of defendant's employees, not on the theories discussed in Privette and its progeny. Even if plaintiff cannot demonstrate that defendant failed to warn of a concealed, pre-existing hazardous condition on the premises or negligently exercised control over plaintiff's work, defendant has not shown that plaintiff cannot successfully assert his premises owner/contractor liability cause of action against it. As to causation, the substantial factor issue was the sole causation issue raised in defendant's Notice of Motion. Defendant failed to put forth undisputed material facts in its Separate Statement to properly raise this issue. Even if defendant properly raised this issue, it failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was, in reasonable medical probability, a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his risk of developing his asbestos-related disease(s). Defendant did not demonstrate that plaintiff failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive written discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support such a claim and this failure precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. As to any issue related to threshold exposure - whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant - such issue was not properly raised by defendant. Any brief statements defendant made on this topic were not included in defendant's Notice of Motion or Separate Statement and only pertained to its argument related to Privette. Assuming that such issue was properly raised, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Although defendant titled its motion as a motion for summary judgment and, in the alternative, summary adjudication of issues, the court treated it only as a motion for summary judgment, based on the arguments made by defendant, defendant's Separate Statement, and CRC 3.1350. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Kimberly R. Hendershott of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12552 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-26-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Gardiner McKleroy of WILSON ELSER MOSKOWITZ EDELMAN & DICKER LLP (415-433-0990), appearing for defendant / moving party Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Michael Rhodes

RULING:
DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE DRYWALL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's causes of action for negligence and strict liability is off calendar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant did not demonstrate that plaintiff failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive written discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support his claim. Defendant did not demonstrate that it sufficiently questioned plaintiff during his deposition regarding the work he saw defendant's employees perform at any time prior to the Hewlett Packard site in 1977. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden by negating an essential element of plaintiff's claim. Even if defendant successfully demonstrated that all Kaiser Gypsum drywall accessory products did not contain asbestos as of 1975, defendant did not demonstrate that its employees never exposed plaintiff to Kaiser Gypsum drywall accessory products prior to 1975. Plaintiff has dismissed his negligence and strict liability causes of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Claudia J. Knap of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #6099 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
